title: Higginsia pyrophylla
taxonomy: Higginsia pyrophylla is a species of sponge, specifically belonging to the phylum Porifera. This classification places it within the kingdom Animalia, as sponges are multicellular and eukaryotic organisms. Higginsia pyrophylla further falls under the class Demospongiae, a group characterized by their cellular composition and lack of a nervous system. At the order level, it is classified as Dictyoceratida, a group of demosponges known for their unique body structure and coloration. This classification indicates Higginsia pyrophylla's position within the larger sponge family.

intro: Higginsia pyrophylla is a species of marine sponge, a type of invertebrate animal that belongs to the phylum Porifera. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing the risk of infections, and alleviating symptoms of certain diseases. The bioactive constituents of Higginsia pyrophylla include pyropheophorbide-a, a photosynthetic pigment with antioxidant properties, as well as other compounds such as triterpenoids and sterols. Higginsia pyrophylla is commonly used in herbal preparations, including extracts and infusions, to support immune function and overall well-being, although further research is needed to fully understand its therapeutic potential.
